task_completion.visual_evidence: The Rocket.Chat main interface is visible in the browser, indicating successful login as the admin user.

task_completion.description: The agent navigated to the Message settings in the Administration panel.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Administration workspace is open, and the 'Message' settings section is selected and visible.

task_completion.description: The agent enabled the bad words filter.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A toggle switch or checkbox for the bad words filter (often labeled 'Allow Bad Words Filter') is in the enabled/on position.

task_completion.description: The agent added the required restricted words to the filter list.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text input field for the bad words filter list contains exactly the words 'classified', 'confidential', 'proprietary', and 'restricted' (typically comma-separated or newline-separated).

task_completion.description: The agent saved the settings configuration.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save changes' button is clicked, or a success toast/notification indicating that the settings were updated successfully is visible.

task_completion.description: The agent verified the filter by posting a test message in the #general channel.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The #general channel is open, and a message sent by the user is visible in the chat history. The message must contain the word 'classified' or its censored equivalent (e.g., asterisks like '**********') demonstrating the filter is active.

integrity.description: The agent used the Rocket.Chat web interface to configure the settings.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the web browser with the Rocket.Chat UI being interacted with, rather than a terminal window executing API curl commands or database scripts.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual Rocket.Chat application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser address bar shows 'localhost:3000' and the standard Rocket.Chat interface is present, not a text editor or a mock UI.

integrity.description: The test message was actually posted to the channel, not just typed.
integrity.visual_evidence: The test message appears in the main chat history area of the #general channel, rather than just sitting unsent in the message composer input box.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The required words to be added to the bad words filter list are exactly: 'classified', 'confidential', 'proprietary', and 'restricted'. The test channel must be '#general'. The internal setting IDs are 'Message_AllowBadWordsFilter' and 'Message_BadWordsFilterList', which correspond to the profanity filter toggle and word list in the UI.
